title: Delivery worker uses non-SSRF-safe reqwest client
description:
LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:119-129

FINDING: `run_worker` builds its own `reqwest::Client` with `redirect::Policy::none()` and a 30s timeout, but does NOT attach the `SsrfSafeResolver` custom DNS resolver that is used by the server's `http_client`. The SSRF-safe client is wired up in `server::serve` (line 116-120) and stored in `AppState.http_client`. The delivery worker runs as a `tokio::spawn` and builds a fresh client without the resolver. The relay `add` and `remove` functions (src/relay.rs:42-45, 212-215) do the same — they build bare clients with no custom resolver. The custom resolver is the primary SSRF defense (per the comment in server.rs:114); the preliminary `is_private_host_resolved` check at delivery time (line 175) is a TOCTOU guard that is bypassable via DNS rebinding because the actual TCP connection uses the standard system resolver.

IMPACT: Delivery to a follower whose inbox domain resolves to a private IP via DNS rebinding (CNAME chained to a TTL-0 A record) bypasses the SSRF guard at the TCP connection stage. An attacker who controls a follower entry in the database (e.g., via a rogue Follow that passes the preliminary check) can trigger HTTP requests to internal services.
close_reason: Fixed: delivery worker client now uses SsrfSafeResolver
